human_verification.notes: Reviewed the package for automotive warranty specificity, VIN and parts-traceability discipline, recall and stop-sale boundaries, authority-source quality, commercialization notes, and validated spec-pack completeness.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary is explicit to automotive warranty execution, repair-order progression, VIN and claim evidence handling, parts and returned-part traceability, blocked-work escalation, and completion evidence.

Authority sources cover FTC warranty and dealer-disclosure guidance, NHTSA warranty-claim and manufacturer-communications baselines, NASTF service-information workflows, AIAG warranty terminology, and GS1 traceability standards relevant to automotive aftersales work.

Evaluation scenarios cover normal in-scope execution, missing prerequisites, unsupported completion, VIN and parts traceability conflicts, recall and stop-sale boundaries, goodwill and financial boundary rejection, rework, supplier substitution escalation, high-volume queue handling, low-confidence escalation, conflicting-policy handling, and tenant-specific EV adaptation.

The package is organized under catalog/naics-overlays/automotive-and-mobility/warranty-operations-specialist/ so automotive warranty operations stay separate from adjacent quality, production planning, inventory, procurement, supplier-quality, and logistics lanes.

review_notes: No unreviewed regressions were identified in the local package review.
Boundary enforcement remained strong around legal remedy interpretation, goodwill approval, supplier substitution approval, recall handling, and unsupported completion.

notes: This package is suitable as a validated warranty operations specialist that supports evidence-backed automotive dealer warranty progression, traceability-aware exception handling, and boundary-safe escalation.
Runtime remains specification-based until a separately promoted retained implementation exists.
